Title: Expansion of a core regulon by transposable elements promotes Arabidopsis chemical diversity and pathogen defense

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antibacterial defense; pathogen defense; promotes plant fitness
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a CYP82C2 enhancer that enables WRKY33-dependent pathogen-inducible CYP82C2 expression and thereby promotes 4OH-ICN biosynthesis and defense
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative promoter analysis; ChIP-PCR; transactivation assays in Nicotiana benthamiana and Arabidopsis cyp82C2 protoplasts; epigenetic signature analysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: present upstream of CYP82C2 in A. thaliana; functions under pathogen/ETI-like induction
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TE-derived LINE retrotransposon exapted as enhancer
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: A. thaliana-specific upstream element of CYP82C2
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be used as a pathogen-inducible cis-regulatory module to engineer species-specific defense metabolite production by rewiring pathway genes into WRKY33 regulon
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: EPCOT3 region is enriched for H3K4me2 and lacks H3K27me3, consistent with open chromatin/active enhancer state enabling WRKY33 binding
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native CYP82C2 promoter containing EPCOT3; pathogen-inducible enhancer context
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: comparative genomics/bioinformatics; published epigenomic datasets for H3K4me2 and H3K27me3
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A. thaliana-specific TE exaptation event; EPCOT3 encompasses the W4 WRKY33-binding region and is sufficient to confer WRKY33 transactivation of CYP82C2
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EPCOT3 is a TE-derived enhancer upstream of CYP82C2. 2. It mediates strong WRKY33 binding and pathogen-responsive CYP82C2 transcription. 3. This rewiring enabled species-specific 4OH-ICN production and enhanced pathogen defense.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antibacterial defense; pathogen defense; contributes to plant fitness
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for 4OH-ICN accumulation and defense; pathogen-induced expression of CYP82C2 correlates with 4OH-ICN production specifically in A. thaliana
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant phenotype from prior pathway assignment referenced in paper; qPCR; ChIP-PCR for upstream WRKY33 binding; transactivation assays with A. thaliana vs A. lyrata loci in N. benthamiana and Arabidopsis protoplasts; comparative metabolite profiling across species
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant for 4OH-ICN formation; regulatory recruitment into WRKY33 regulon is key for pathway activation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: strongly induced by Psta/pathogen effectors in A. thaliana; unchanged under iron deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: in A. thaliana induced by pathogen; in A. lyrata ortholog not pathogen-induced
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native CYP82C2 promoter with EPCOT3 enhancer
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: comparative genomics/synteny; metabolite profiling; published co-expression/transcript data referenced
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CYP82C2 likely duplicated from CYP82C4 and underwent regulatory neofunctionalization; protein sequence remains under purifying selection and likely retains catalytic similarity to CYP82C4
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering pathogen-inducible 4OH-ICN production, especially with cognate enhancer/regulator modules such as EPCOT3 and WRKY33
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP82C2 catalyzes the final hydroxylation step from ICN to 4OH-ICN. 2. Its pathogen-induced expression is specific to A. thaliana and correlates with species-specific 4OH-ICN accumulation. 3. Recruitment of CYP82C2 into the WRKY33 regulon underlies regulatory neofunctionalization of this pathway gene.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: WRKY33 directly activates FOX1 expression during pathogen response as part of the 4OH-ICN pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR in WT, wrky33, and WRKY33-complemented lines; ChIP-PCR of upstream W-box region
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: flavin-dependent oxidase / berberine bridge enzyme-like protein (AtBBE3)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: converts ICY toward ICN in the 4OH-ICN pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: upregulated in response to Psta; reduced in wrky33 and restored by WRKY33 complementation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: WRKY33 binds strongly to upstream W2 region containing W-box and WRKY33-specific motifs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate component for pathway stacking to enhance 4OH-ICN production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FOX1 is a WRKY33 target in the 4OH-ICN pathway. 2. Its pathogen-induced expression depends on WRKY33. 3. It contributes precursor formation for 4OH-ICN biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: antibacterial defense; pathogen defense; promotes plant fitness
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential activator of pathogen-induced 4OH-ICN biosynthesis; core regulator expanding an ancient defense regulon
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required to activate 4OH-ICN and camalexin biosynthesis in response to ETI-like pathogen elicitation; natural loss-of-function variation reduces these metabolites and increases pathogen susceptibility
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional activation of tryptophan-derived defense metabolite biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of WRKY33 function decreases camalexin and 4OH-ICN and increases susceptibility to Pst
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value regulator for engineering inducible defense metabolite production, but deployment should preserve proper transactivation domain function and defense-growth balance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant analysis; dex-inducible complementation with WRKY33-flag/myc; qPCR; ChIP-PCR; metabolite profiling; pathogen growth assays; natural variation analysis in accession Di-G
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: group I WRKY transcription factor with WRKY DNA-binding domains and C-terminal transactivation region
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: accession resequencing comparison among 35 accessions; transcript/metabolite co-expression analyses from Supplementary Tables
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: direct transcriptional activation via W-box-containing promoter/enhancer regions
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ETI-like pathogen effector responses; Pst avrRpm1; Pst avrRps4; Pst avrRpt2; chitosan at high concentration
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates CYP82C2 and FOX1 directly; activates CYP71A12, CYP71A13 and CYP71B15-associated defense pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: DNA regions containing W-boxes and WRKY33-specific motifs; FLAG/myc-tagged WRKY33 constructs used experimentally
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Di-G accession carries an early nonsense mutation in WRKY33 associated with low camalexin/4OH-ICN and higher ICN; larger myc tag partially impaired transactivation of CYP82C2, implicating WRKY33 C-terminus in activation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: seedlings and leaves under pathogen challenge
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WRKY33 is required for pathogen-induced 4OH-ICN and camalexin biosynthesis. 2. It directly binds upstream of FOX1 and CYP82C2 and activates their transcription. 3. Natural or induced loss of WRKY33 reduces defense metabolites and increases bacterial susceptibility.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: DEX-inducible pTA7002 system used for complementation; native target promoters/enhancers in Arabidopsis
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Its activity on CYP82C2 depends on accessible chromatin at EPCOT3 enhancer; no direct epigenetic regulation of WRKY33 itself reported
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Psta and other ETI elicitors; dex induction in complementation assays
